ab: mwadringadringai nomi makanaelre, ai iloka ko nomiya mo cekele ki’elaelra ki tarwarodranga.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去部落訪問耆老。
en: A: Hello everyone, today I’m taking you to the tribe to interview the elders.

ab: eene, ai sin Taromaka nai kikawriva ki tarwarodrang.
ch: B:好喔!我們要跟耆老說魯凱語。
en: B:Okay! We’re going to say Ngudradrekay to the seniors.

ab: wa kelakealanga ta.
en: A: Are we almost there?

ab: kelakelanga ta.
en: B:Almost there.

ab: naomo, wa kela nai ceele mosowa.
en: A: Grandpa, we’ve come to see you.

ab: ya! wa thingale nomi so Taromaka, matharirir aramoro.
ch: B:你們會說魯凱語啊!真是太棒了!
en: B:  You speak Tarumake! That’s great! *Please change the language to your native language.

ab: mwadringadringai nomi makanaelre, ai iloka ko nomiya mwa tawbecenga twatoma.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去小米田工作。
en: A:Hello everyone, today I will take you to work in the millet field.

ab: eene, sinsi
en: B: Yes, sir.

ab: niyama tilribi makanaelre, waolringaw ta iloko na satomatomana.
ch: A:大家過來排隊，記得帶好工具。
en: A: Come and line up, remember to bring your tools.

ab: eene, sinsi, wa iloko nai ina sarokorokodro.
ch: B:是的，老師，我們都拿好工具了。
en: B: Yes, sir, we’ve got our tools.

ab: wa kelakelanga ta?
en: A: Are we there yet?

ab: iniyana, wa dailiyana.
en: B: Not yet, it’s still far away.

ab: mwa wacilay.
en: A: You go watering.

ab: eene, nawa wacilay.
en: B: Okay, I’ll water it.

ab: mwa malra na sasisawsawa.
en: A: You go get the bird catcher.

ab: eene, namwa sisaw na azazame.
en: B: Okay, I’ll go and catch the birds.

ab: mwadringadringai nomi makanaelre, ai yowa ta tala’ola’owa dra la golagolay kaivaiyana.
ch: A:大家好，我們今天要去動物園。
en: A: Hello everyone, we are going to the zoo today.

ab: eene mathariri, ai ikakwa ta mowa.
ch: B:好棒喔!我們要怎麼去呢?
en: B: Awesome! How are we going to get there?

ab: mobeleng ta sa baase.
en: A: Let’s take the bus.

ab: niyama kikai tilribi, ai langaynga ta sa kipo.
ch: A:大家排好隊，我們準備買票。
en: A: Everyone line up, we are ready to buy tickets.

ab: eene.
en: B: Yes.

ab: kai cocobonga ka lazazame, kai lrikodranga ka laolawlay, angikamaniya siyavava.
ch: A:前面是鳥類，後面是蛇類，大家自己參觀。
en: A: There are birds in the front and snakes in the back.

ab: sinsi mwakolodra ko na olawlay.
en: B: I’m afraid of snakes.

ab: kadro kitoma, mwa ceele dra azazame.
en: A: It doesn’t matter, then you go and watch the birds.

ab: lo lribatenga ki eea ka toki, wakela ta irobo kikai.
ch: A:我們一個小時後回來集合。
en: A: We’ll be back in an hour.

ab: eene, maelanenga sinsi.
en: B: Yes, thank you.

ab: manema ka wa ceele so kaivaiyana?
en: A: What did you see today?

ab: wa ceela ko makalra ka olawlay, si makalra ka azazame.
ch: B:我看到了好多蛇，還有好多鳥。
en: B: I saw a lot of snakes and a lot of birds.

ab: kirarokodra, wadavadavacenga nai.
en: A: Hurry up, we have to go.

ab: adrawai li, mabalrebalre kai dra’ale li.
en: B: Wait for me, my feet are tired.

ab: ila ta karedede mwa lwangolangoy.
en: A: Shall we go swimming together?

ab: eene, ila.
en: B:Yes! Let’s go!

ab: takalra kai omase yakai kikai lwangolangoy.
en: A: There are a lot of people swimming here.

ab: madraw si mwadringi kwani acilay kawai, ta ikai kikai langolangoy.
ch: B:那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
en: B: The water is deep there, so we just swim here.
